Pedro Ruiz de Azagra

Primer señor de Albarracin
memoryArtifacts.description: Pedro Ruiz (Rodríguez) de Azagra (muerto en 1186) fue un noble y hombre de armas navarro que se estableció en el señorío independiente de Albarracín, el cual permaneció bajo esta condición hasta 1284. …

zh.lifeSummary: Pedro Ruíz de Azagra, I Señor de Albarracín y II Señoror de Estella是Don Pedro Muñoz Corna和Toda López de Alagón的兒子，出生。他於大約1122年與Toda Pérez de Arazuri在西班牙結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1186年11月18日在西班牙阿拉格昂特盧爾阿爾瓦拉希阿爾瓦拉希。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Pedro Ruíz de Azagra, I Señor de Albarracín y II Señoror de Estella是Don Pedro Muñoz Corna和Toda López de Alagón的儿子，出生。他于大约1122年与Toda Pérez de Arazuri在西班牙结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1186年11月18日在西班牙阿拉格昂特盧爾阿爾瓦拉希阿爾瓦拉希。

nameMeanings.name: Ruíz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Jesus, Francisco, Luis, Miguel, Roberto, Pedro, Ramon, Jorge.
Spanish and Portuguese: patronymic from the personal name Ruy, a short form of Rodrigo .

variants.name: Ruisi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Santo, Angelo, Matteo, Carmela, Lorenzo, Pasquale, Vittorio.
Italian (Sicily): probably an Italianized form of Spanish   Ruiz  .

variants.name: Ruz
meanings: Spanish: variant of   Ruiz  .

variants.name: Rodrigo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Elena, Rafael, Alfonso, Alicia, Angel, Araceli, Benardo, Carlos, Carlota, Cristina, Emilio.  Indian Devi, Dileep, Dilip, Gamini, Mahendra, Manoj, Sunil, Tilak.

Spanish and Portuguese: from the personal name Rodrigo, from ancient Germanic Hrōdrīc, a compound of hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + rīc ‘power, kingdom’. Rodrigo is one of the most important Spanish personal names of ancient Germanic origin. It was borne by the last of the Visigoth kings and by a Christian saint, Saint Roderick, one of the 9th-century martyrs of Córdoba. This surname is also found in and around some of the former Portuguese territories of western India.

variants.name: Ruis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Arturo, Carlos, Conception, Elba, Fernando, Jose, Josue, Juan, Jubenal, Lazaro, Lupe.
Spanish and Portuguese: variant of   Ruiz  .

variants.name: Dey

English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Albarracín
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Spanish: habitational name from the town of Albarracín in the province of Teruel, which is of Arabic origin.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: Estella
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Spanish: habitational name from Estella in the province of Navarre. The placename is probably a result of mistranslation of its Basque name Lizarra (from lizarr ‘ash tree’) into Latin as if it were derived from izarra ‘star’ (Latin stella).

nameMeanings.name: Pedro
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Greek. See Peter .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: Pedro Ruíz de Azagra, I Señor de Albarracín y II Señoror de Estella was born as the son of Don Pedro Muñoz Corna and Toda López de Alagón. He married Toda Pérez de Arazuri about 1122, in Spain. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 4 daughters. He died on 18 November 1186, in Albarracín, Albarracín, Teruel, Aragón, Spain.

fr.lifeSummary: Pedro Ruíz de Azagra, I Señor de Albarracín y II Señoror d’Estella est né, fils de Don Pedro Muñoz Corna et Toda López de Alagón. Il a épousé Toda Pérez de Arazuri environ 1122, à Espagne. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 18 novembre 1186, à Albarracín, Albarracín, Teruel, Aragón, Espagne.

de.lifeSummary: Pedro Ruíz de Azagra, I Señor de Albarracín y II Señoror de Estella wurde, als Sohn von Don Pedro Muñoz Corna und Toda López de Alagón geboren. Er hat um 1122 in Spanien Toda Pérez de Arazuri geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 18. November 1186 in Albarracín, Albarracín, Teruel, Aragón, Spanien.

it.lifeSummary: Pedro Ruíz de Azagra, I Señor de Albarracín y II Señoror de Estella è nato il come figlio di Don Pedro Muñoz Corna e Toda López de Alagón. Lui sposato Toda Pérez de Arazuri circa 1122, nel Spagna. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 4 figlie. Morì il 18 novembre 1186, nel Albarracín, Albarracín, Teruel, Aragón, Spagna.

ja.lifeSummary: Pedro Ruíz de Azagra, I Señor de Albarracín y II Señoror de EstellaはDon Pedro Muñoz CornaとToda López de Alagónの息子として生まれた。約1122年にスペインでToda Pérez de Arazuriと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1186年11月18日にスペインアラグoンテルエルアルバーラクiンアルバーラクiンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Pedro Ruíz de Azagra, I Señor de Albarracín y II Señoror de Estella(은)는 Don Pedro Muñoz Corna(와)과 Toda López de Alagón의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1122년 스페인에서 Toda Pérez de Arazuri(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1186년 11월 18일 스페인 아라그o 터얼 알바라ci 알바라ci에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Pedro Ruíz de Azagra, I Señor de Albarracín y II Señoror de Estella nasceu filho de Don Pedro Muñoz Corna e Toda López de Alagón. Ele casou-se com Toda Pérez de Arazuri aproximadamente 1122, em Espanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 18 de novembro de 1186, em Albarracín, Albarracín, Teruel, Aragón, Espanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Pedro Ruíz de Azagra, I Señor de Albarracín y II Señoror de Estella родился. Его родителей звали Don Pedro Muñoz Corna и Toda López de Alagón. Он женился на женщине по имени Toda Pérez de Arazuri около 1122 г. в местности Испания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 4 дочери. Он умер 18 ноября 1186 г. в местности албарракiн, албарракiн, тэруел, арагoн, Испания.

es.lifeSummary: Pedro Ruíz de Azagra, I Señor de Albarracín y II Señoror de Estella nació como hijo de Don Pedro Muñoz Corna y Toda López de Alagón. Se casó con Toda Pérez de Arazuri aproximadamente 1122, en España. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 4 hijas. Murió el 18 de noviembre de 1186, en Albarracín, Albarracín, Teruel, Aragón, España.
